body_txt: So you have a character in the group who is nature-savvy. Maybe they are an Elf or Magical Native American or just some gruff Wild Man or Ranger-type . One way to establish that character as being badass and not the Granola Girl is to show them to be a good tracker. Of course, any moron can follow footprints in the mud. Since our character is so good, they'll not only be able to tell you how many people there were, but any of the following also:  There are two ways the Scarily Competent Tracker works their magic. The first is to crouch and prod the footprints with their fingers. The other is to stick their ear to the ground and listen. Or possibly The Nose Knows , but that usually goes into Super-Senses . If the Tracker is part of The Team , then they typically fill the role of The Smart Guy . See also Sherlock Scan , Hyper-Awareness and Tracking Spell . May be represented via Fluorescent Footprints . Compare They Have the Scent! , with which this trope can overlap. If the Tracker is non-sapient, then you may have a Super-Persistent Predator on your hands. Good luck with that.
